This is article 3:

Article title: Spokesman of the Hong Kong and Macao Affairs Office of the State Council Made a Statement on the Violent Incident of Storming the Legislative Council in Hong Kong

Article text: On 2 July, the Spokesman of the Hong Kong and Macao Affairs Office of the State Council issued a statement regarding the violent storm at the Legislative Council of the Hong Kong SAR, expressing his firm support to the HKSAR Government and the police in handling the incident in accordance with the law, and investigating the perpetrators of the violence and holding them criminally responsible in accordance with the law.

The spokesman said that on July 1, the day when people from all walks of life in Hong Kong commemorated Hong Kong's return to the motherland and the establishment of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region, some extremist radicals stormed the Legislative Council building in an extremely violent manner and deliberately damaged the Legislative Council's facilities under the pretext of opposing the review of the law by the SAR government. Such serious offences trample on Hong Kong's rule of law, destroy Hong Kong's social order, damage Hong Kong's fundamental interests, and are a blatant challenge to the principle of "one country, two systems".

The spokesman said that the Central Government resolutely supports the HKSAR Government and the police in discharging their responsibilities in accordance with the law, and supports the respective HKSAR authorities in prosecuting the criminal responsibility of the perpetrators of violenc …[truncated]

Mathematics within Christian philosophy
The fall of the Roman empire implied that Greek manuscripts became buried in the libraries of
small monasteries. The number of people being able to read Greek diminished. The pax romana
had secured a wide spread of Greek philosophy, and of Greek philosophical manuscripts, and
although it had reached only a tiny part of the population, it was kept alive, in a way. Where geometria
and arithmetica, even if mostly viewed practical or mystical, had been a part of Roman
culture, the early Christians distanced themselves from the pagan Greek philosophers. The ideas
behind the Greek world order were not consistent with the notion of an omnipotent God. The
new Christian ideas proved to be much more supportive for mathematical practices, since the
Christian God was a deity who favoured rules and laws, instead of the irrational and quarrelling
gods of the antique civilisations. The divine rules and laws reflected favourable upon the ideas
within geometria or arithmetica, and although there was no systematic use of mathematical
knowledge within theology, there were several of the old Christian thinkers who tried to connect
the two fields. We will have a closer look at two of them.
One of the reasons for the popularity of Saint Augustine (354 - 430) was the fact that he was autodidact.
It was exactly the fact that he was not versed in the Greek philosophers, which made
him the ideal icon to the young religion. Later generations would consider him one of the church
fathers, although he had been quite profane in his youth (“do gr …[truncated]
